explanation: Nonapeptide-1 is a synthetic peptide that inhibits alpha-MSH (melanocyte-stimulating hormone) receptor signaling to reduce melanin production in melanocytes, providing a targeted brightening effect at the receptor level. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and used in brightening formulas for its tyrosinase pathway-interrupting approach to pigmentation reduction.

Nordihydroguaiaretic acid (NDGA) is a plant-derived polyphenolic antioxidant from the creosote bush with potent free-radical-scavenging and anti-inflammatory properties, and research-backed sebum-regulation and anti-androgenic activity relevant to acne-prone skin. It is generally well-tolerated and used in anti-aging and blemish-targeting formulas for its combined antioxidant and sebum-modulating properties.

Nylon-12 is a synthetic polyamide powder used in cosmetics as a soft-focus texture agent and slip enhancer that creates a smooth, velvety surface on the skin. It is considered inert, non-sensitizing, and well-tolerated — functioning as a sensory and texture modifier rather than a skin-active ingredient.

Nymphaea alba (white water lily) flower extract provides flavonoid antioxidants including quercetin and kaempferol with free-radical-scavenging, anti-inflammatory, and skin-brightening properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and brightening formulas for its antioxidant content.

O-cymen-5-ol (isopropyl methylphenol, IPMP) is a phenolic preservative and antimicrobial agent with potent activity against bacteria and yeast used to extend formula shelf life and provide mild deodorizing benefits. It is considered well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations and used in natural-positioned preservative systems.

Ocimum sanctum (holy basil/tulsi) leaf extract is an Ayurvedic adaptogenic botanical rich in ursolic acid, rosmarinic acid, and flavonoids with potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. It is generally well-tolerated at extract concentrations and used in soothing and protective formulas for its broad-spectrum antioxidant and antibacterial content.

Octinoxate (ethylhexyl methoxycinnamate) is the world's most widely used chemical UVB sunscreen filter, providing effective protection against burning rays but raising concerns about contact sensitization, potential endocrine-disrupting activity at systemic levels, and environmental harm to coral reef ecosystems. It is photounstable without stabilizers and can cause contact and photocontact dermatitis on reactive or sensitive skin.

Octocrylene is a chemical UVB sunscreen filter that also stabilizes other photolabile UV filters including avobenzone, improving the overall photostability of sunscreen formulas. It has been flagged for potential endocrine-disrupting activity at systemic levels, can cause contact and photocontact dermatitis on reactive skin, and is a known sensitizer — particularly for individuals with benzophenone sensitivity due to cross-reactivity.

Octyldodecanol is a branched-chain fatty alcohol used as a lightweight, non-greasy emollient that enhances spreadability and skin feel in formulas. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and used in moisturizers, sunscreens, and color cosmetics as a comfortable texture modifier.

Octyldodecyl shea butterate is an ester derived from octyldodecanol and shea butter fatty acids, providing a lightweight, non-greasy emollient with better spreadability than raw shea butter. It is well-tolerated and used in moisturizers and lip products for its comfortable texture and shea-derived fatty acid conditioning.

Octyldodecyl stearoyl stearate is a rich polyfunctional ester emollient providing excellent skin adhesion, a silky feel, and good spreadability in lip products and anhydrous formulas. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and used for its luxurious texture and good color payoff in color cosmetics.

Oenothera biennis (evening primrose) flower extract provides polyphenol antioxidants with free-radical-scavenging and skin-conditioning properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and soothing formulas for its antioxidant and skin-nourishing content.

Oenothera biennis (evening primrose) oil is uniquely rich in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA, ~10%) — an omega-6 fatty acid with potent anti-inflammatory properties that is deficient in eczema-prone and atopic skin. It is well-tolerated and particularly valued for dry, atopic, and inflamed skin for its barrier-repairing and anti-inflammatory GLA content, though its high unsaturated fatty acid content makes it prone to oxidation.

Oenothera biennis (evening primrose) oil — the asterisk indicates organic certification — is uniquely rich in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA, ~10%) with potent anti-inflammatory and barrier-repairing properties particularly valued for dry, atopic, and inflamed skin. Its high unsaturated fatty acid content makes it prone to oxidation — freshness and sealed, opaque packaging are important quality indicators.

Olea europaea (olive) fruit oil is rich in oleic acid (~70%) and squalene with emollient and antioxidant properties. Its high oleic acid content can be disruptive to the linoleic acid-balanced barrier in atopic or eczema-prone skin, making high-linoleic alternatives like sunflower or rosehip oil a better choice for reactive skin; it has moderate comedogenic potential at higher concentrations.

Olea europaea (olive) husk oil is extracted from olive pomace and provides oleic acid, squalene, and tocopherols with emollient and antioxidant properties. It shares the fatty acid profile and moderate comedogenic potential of standard olive oil and is generally well-tolerated by most skin types.

Olea europea (olive) fruit oil — an alternate spelling of Olea europaea — is rich in oleic acid (~70%) and squalene with emollient and antioxidant properties. Its high oleic acid content makes it less suitable for eczema-prone or highly reactive skin; it has moderate comedogenic potential at higher concentrations.

Oleanolic acid is a pentacyclic triterpenoid found in olive leaves, garlic, and other plants with documented anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and mild tyrosinase-inhibiting brightening properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and brightening formulas for its combined antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity.

Oleic acid is an 18-carbon monounsaturated omega-9 fatty acid — the most abundant fatty acid in human sebum — used as an emollient and penetration enhancer in skincare. Its high oleic content can be disruptive to the barrier in atopic and eczema-prone skin (which are deficient in linoleic acid), making it less suitable than high-linoleic oils for reactive or barrier-compromised skin; it has moderate comedogenic potential.

Olive squalene is the unsaturated precursor to squalane derived from olive oil, providing emollient benefits and excellent skin compatibility as a structural analog to human sebum. It is well-tolerated and non-comedogenic, though less shelf-stable than hydrogenated squalane due to oxidation susceptibility — it requires effective antioxidant co-ingredients or protective packaging.

Orbignya oleifera (babassu palm) seed oil is a light, non-greasy tropical plant oil with a fatty acid profile similar to coconut oil (high lauric acid) but with better skin absorption and lower comedogenic potential. It is well-tolerated by most skin types and used in lightweight moisturizers and hair care formulas for its emollient and conditioning properties.

Origanum vulgare (oregano) extract provides carvacrol, thymol, and rosmarinic acid with potent antioxidant and broad-spectrum antimicrobial properties. The extract form is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations and used in purifying and protective formulas for its antimicrobial and antioxidant content.

Origanum vulgare (oregano) leaf extract provides carvacrol, thymol, and rosmarinic acid with potent antioxidant and broad-spectrum antimicrobial properties. It is generally well-tolerated at extract concentrations and used in purifying and protective formulas for its antimicrobial and antioxidant content.

Oryza sativa (rice) bran oil is rich in oleic acid, linoleic acid, and gamma-oryzanol — a unique antioxidant and UV-absorbing compound — with emollient, antioxidant, and mild photoprotective properties. It is well-tolerated, has low comedogenic potential, and is widely used in East Asian skincare for its nourishing and brightening properties.

Oryza sativa (rice) cera (rice bran wax) is a natural plant wax derived from the outer bran of rice, used as a structuring agent and thickener in balms, lip products, and anhydrous formulas. It is considered inert, well-tolerated, and provides a smooth, non-tacky texture.

Oryza sativa (rice) extract provides gamma-oryzanol, ferulic acid, vitamin E, and polysaccharides with antioxidant, brightening, and skin-conditioning properties widely used in East Asian skincare. It is exceptionally well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and used in brightening and protective formulas for its antioxidant density and ferulic acid content.

Ozokerite is a naturally occurring mineral wax (fossilized hydrocarbon) used as a structuring agent and thickener in lip balms, eye pencils, and anhydrous formulas. It is considered inert, non-sensitizing, and well-tolerated, providing a firm, waxy texture without significant skin activity.

Palmitic acid is a 16-carbon saturated fatty acid — one of the most abundant in human skin — used as an emollient and barrier-supporting ingredient in skincare. It is generally well-tolerated, though its high saturate content gives it moderate comedogenic potential, making it a less suitable emollient choice for acne-prone or congestion-prone skin at higher concentrations.

Palmitoyl dipeptide-10 is a palmitoylated signal peptide used in anti-aging formulas to stimulate extracellular matrix proteins and support skin firmness and elasticity. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and used in firming serums and creams for its collagen-stimulating activity.

Palmitoyl hexapeptide-12 is a palmitoylated signal peptide used in anti-aging formulas to stimulate fibroblast activity and promote collagen and elastin synthesis for improved skin firmness and resilience. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and used in firming and anti-aging serums for its extracellular matrix-stimulating properties.
